subject: English
text: Over long periods of time, layers of sediments builds up to a height of a few kilometers.
options: up to a height of a few kilometers.
explanation: The subject 'layers' is plural, so the verb should be 'build' instead of 'builds' to maintain subject-verb agreement.

subject: English
text: When a gas is cooled down it turns into a liquid from a process called condensation.
options: When a gas is cooled down
explanation: The correct preposition is 'by'. It should be 'by a process called condensation'.

subject: English
text: If you want to know the news you can read a newspaper.
explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct.

subject: English
text: Columbus made his first voyage from Europe to America on 1492.
explanation: Years are preceded by the preposition 'in'. It should be 'in 1492'.

subject: English
text: Whenever the sky is clear, you can see the stars in the night.
explanation: The correct idiomatic phrase is 'at night'.

subject: English
text: I'm not working tomorrow, so I don't had to get up early.
explanation: The correct auxiliary verb for the present/future context is 'have'. It should be 'don't have to'.

subject: English
text: She didn't tell anybody about her plans.
explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct.

subject: English
text: She wouldn't have has an accident, if she had driven carefully.
options: She wouldn't have has an accident,
explanation: The past participle form of 'have' must be used. It should be 'wouldn't have had'.

subject: English
text: I will watch film if I finish the work in time.
explanation: 'Film' is a singular countable noun and needs an article. It should be 'watch a film'.

subject: English
text: In 1989, the government did an about-face and Iran restored it's family planning program.
options: In 1989, the government did an about-face
explanation: The possessive pronoun 'its' should be used, not the contraction 'it's' (it is).

subject: English
text: During the pandemic the indigent people had to suffer a lot.
explanation: 'Indigent' means poor or needy. A synonym is 'very poor'.

subject: English
text: She had no idea what made him angry in one minute and jovial the next.
explanation: 'Jovial' means cheerful and friendly. 'Mirthful' is the closest synonym.

subject: English
text: It is sheer lunacy to drive a car in this frosty weather.
explanation: 'Lunacy' refers to extreme folly or eccentricity. 'Insanity' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: Operating on a child with cancer needs meticulous planning and teamwork.
explanation: 'Meticulous' means showing great attention to detail. 'Scrupulous' is a synonym.

subject: English
text: The thrill of over-speeding the vehicle can be exhilarating, but it is important not to take the consequences lightly.
explanation: 'Exhilarating' means making one feel very happy or thrilled. 'Exciting' is the nearest meaning.

subject: English
text: The redemption will now depend on his new strategy of inclusiveness.
explanation: 'Redemption' means the action of saving or being saved. 'Retrieval' is the closest contextual synonym.

subject: English
text: Only three candidates are now in contention for the title.
explanation: Being 'in contention' means having a good chance of success in a contest.

subject: English
text: All my fishing paraphernalia is in the car.
explanation: 'Paraphernalia' refers to miscellaneous articles, especially equipment needed for a particular activity. 'Accessories' fits best.

subject: English
text: The public watched in astonishment as he took a sudden jump from the bridge.
explanation: 'Astonishment' means great surprise. 'Wonderment' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: Drinking inordinate amount of liquor is not good for health.
explanation: 'Inordinate' means unusually or disproportionately large. 'Exorbitant' is a synonym.

subject: English
text: P: because I feel you remember it better when you write something, / Q: I make at least three drafts of a song / R: [Fragmented text from source] / S: [Fragmented text from source]
explanation: The logical flow connects the action (making drafts) with the reason (remembering better).

subject: English
text: P: for hours in the shop / Q: a gunman who held / R: a hostage demanded ransom of 10 lakh / S: [Fragmented text from source]
explanation: Sequence builds appropriately: 'A gunman who held a hostage for hours in the shop demanded ransom of 10 lakh.'

subject: English
text: P: Ukraine has urged its citizens to leave the / Q: living in Russia country immediately / R: [Fragmented text from source] / S: [Fragmented text from source]
explanation: Sentence structuring flows to combine 'Ukraine has urged its citizens' with the 'leaving' context.

subject: English
text: P: ripping roofs off houses and raising fears of / Q: cyclone Emnati crashed into the southeastern / R: coast of Madagascar in the early hours, / S: flooding and food shortages in the region
explanation: Logical sequence describes the cyclone hitting the coast, then ripping roofs off, and finally causing fears of floods.

subject: English
text: P: ecology is protected and aquatic life thrives / Q: we need to utilize the resources of water / R: for different purposes while ensuring that its natural / S: [Fragmented text from source]
explanation: Syntax completes the thought of utilizing water resources responsibly.

subject: English
text: P: very difficult / Q: the poor visibility made the movement of traffic / R: [Fragmented text from source] / S: due to fog
explanation: Proper sequence reads: 'The poor visibility due to fog made the movement of traffic very difficult.'

subject: English
text: P: avenues for the patients to indict / Q: but rarely is it the other way round / R: there are multiple legal / S: the doctors
explanation: The order is 'There are multiple legal avenues for the patients to indict the doctors but rarely is it the other way round.'

subject: English
text: P: flight, and each person / Q: makes his or / R: her own journey / S: life is a solo
explanation: The order is 'Life is a solo flight, and each person makes his or her own journey.'

subject: English
text: P: made it evolve / Q: technologies and equipment has / R: into a rare centre of excellence / S: adoption of the latest
explanation: The order is 'Adoption of the latest technologies and equipment has made it evolve into a rare centre of excellence.'

subject: English
text: P: please mark the same / Q: be made in response to this notice / R: if you have any complaint to / S: to the concerned authority
explanation: The order is 'If you have any complaint to be made in response to this notice please mark the same to the concerned authority.'

subject: English
text: The parental support can fortify a child's learning.
explanation: 'Fortify' means to strengthen. The opposite is 'weaken'.

subject: English
text: Now we can have another dazzling thought, as an outcome of my brilliant research!
explanation: 'Dazzling' implies bright or impressive. The opposite is 'murky' (dark and gloomy).

subject: English
text: The group held some clandestine meetings to resolve the matter.
explanation: 'Clandestine' means kept secret. The opposite is 'public'.

subject: English
text: In the winters, the countryside view is bleak and the house is drafty.
explanation: 'Bleak' means bare, dreary or lacking vegetation. 'Verdant' (green with grass/vegetation) is its antonym.

subject: English
text: Amid commotion the leader and his supporters remained passive.
explanation: 'Commotion' implies a state of confused and noisy disturbance. 'Quietude' is the opposite.

subject: English
text: He had the audacity to blame him.
explanation: 'Audacity' implies rude or disrespectful bold behavior. 'Politeness' is an antonym.

subject: English
text: They are making plans for the abatement of the nuisance within a specified time.
explanation: 'Abatement' means the reduction or lessening of something. 'Accumulation' is its opposite.

subject: English
text: There is exponential growth in the development of the scientific researches in the recent few decades.
explanation: 'Exponential' means becoming more and more rapid/large. 'Meager' is the closest opposite meaning small/inadequate.

subject: English
text: He was known for his sagacity.
explanation: 'Sagacity' means the quality of being sage or having good judgment. 'Ignorance' is an antonym.

subject: English
text: The river Ganges culminates in the Bay of Bengal.
explanation: 'Culminates' implies reaching an end or conclusion. The opposite is 'originates'.

subject: English
text: The lion's share
options: The largest and the best part of something
explanation: 'The lion's share' refers to the largest part of something.

subject: English
text: Spill the beans
options: Take offence at someone's rude behaviour
explanation: To 'spill the beans' means to reveal secret information unintentionally or indiscreetly.

subject: English
text: Cook someone's goose
explanation: To 'cook someone's goose' means to ruin someone's plans or chances of success.

subject: English
text: Disappear into thin air
explanation: To 'disappear into thin air' implies vanishing suddenly and completely.

subject: English
text: Put the screws on
options: Forcing someone to do what you want
Using someone's trick for own benefit
explanation: To 'put the screws on' someone means to exert pressure on them to do what is required.

subject: English
text: Sit on the fence
explanation: To 'sit on the fence' means to avoid making a decision or choosing a side in an argument.

subject: English
text: Be as sharp as a tack
explanation: Being 'as sharp as a tack' means being very intelligent, clever, or quick-witted.

subject: English
text: From pillar to post
options: Keep moving from one place to another
Jumping from the top of the mountain
explanation: To be driven 'from pillar to post' means to be forced to keep moving from one place to another.

subject: English
text: Heath Robinson
options: Very complicated system or machine for doing a simple task
Treating everyone as enemy, when they are not
Very lazy person, slow in action
explanation: 'Heath Robinson' refers to a machine or system that is absurdly complex for a simple task, named after the British cartoonist.

subject: English
text: As thick as thieves
options: Someone who uses everyone for personal gain
Very close friends who are looked upon with suspicion
explanation: To be 'as thick as thieves' means to be very close friends and sharing secrets.

subject: Physics
text: Two identical spring balances S1 and S2 are connected one after the other and are held vertically as shown in the figure. A mass of 10 kg is hanging from S2. If the readings on S1 and S2 are W1 and W2 respectively, then:
explanation: Both springs must support the 10 kg mass below them (assuming massless springs), so both will read 10 kg.

subject: Physics
text: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s^2 and neglecting air resistance will give:
explanation: Time to hit ground: t = sqrt(2h/g) = sqrt(2*20/10) = 2 s. Distance R = v*t = 12 * 2 = 24 m.

subject: Physics
text: A sphere of volume V is made of a material with lower density than water. While on Earth, it floats on water with its volume f1V (f1 < 1) submerged. On the other hand, on a spaceship accelerating with acceleration a < g (g is the acceleration due to gravity on Earth) in outer space, its submerged volume in water is f2V. Then:
explanation: The fraction submerged depends on the ratio of densities (solid/liquid), which is independent of the effective gravitational acceleration.

subject: Physics
text: Two identical containers X and Y are connected at the bottom by a thin tube of negligible volume. The tube has a valve in it, as shown in the figure. Initially container X has a liquid filled up to height h in it and container Y is empty. When the valve is opened, both containers have equal amount of liquid in equilibrium. If the initial (before the valve is opened) potential energy of the liquid is P1 and the final potential energy is P2 then
explanation: Initial PE: Mg(h/2). Final PE: (M/2)g(h/4) + (M/2)g(h/4) = Mgh/4. Thus P1 = 2 P2.

subject: Physics
text: A particle is moving in a circle of radius R with constant speed v. Its average acceleration over the time when it moves over half the circle is:
explanation: Change in velocity = 2v. Time = pi*R/v. Average acceleration = Delta(v)/t = 2v / (pi*R/v) = 2v^2 / (pi*R).

subject: Physics
text: Two forces of 5.0 N each are acting on a point mass. If the angle between the forces is 60 degrees, then the net force acting on the point mass has magnitude close to:
explanation: Resultant = sqrt(F1^2 + F2^2 + 2*F1*F2*cos(60)) = sqrt(25+25+25) = sqrt(75) ≈ 8.66 N.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is not an igneous rock?
explanation: Slate is a metamorphic rock formed from shale or mudstone. The others are igneous.

subject: Geography
text: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Hypocenter is the point on the surface of the Earth, nearest to the focus. 2. Velocity of earthquake waves is higher in denser materials. 3. P waves move faster and are the first to arrive at the surface of the Earth.
explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect (the Epicenter is on the surface, Hypocenter is the focus inside the earth). Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

subject: Geography
text: In terms of geological time scale, the quaternary period consists of two epochs. They are:
explanation: The Quaternary period consists of the Pleistocene and Holocene epochs.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of arrangement of the given planets in descending order of their density (in gm/cm^3)?
explanation: Earth (5.51) > Venus (5.24) > Jupiter (1.33) > Saturn (0.69).

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is not a cold current?
explanation: The Eastern Australian Current is a warm ocean current.

subject: Geography
text: The process of Podsolization is predominantly found in:
explanation: Podsolization is dominant in the cool, humid climates characteristic of Taiga (boreal) forests.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Joint Military exercise 'Keen Sword 23' was conducted between:
explanation: Exercise Keen Sword is a joint military exercise held between the USA and Japan.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The Battle of Rezang La, an epic battle in hostile conditions, was fought by the Indian Army in:
explanation: The Battle of Rezang La was fought during the Sino-Indian War of 1962.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Consider the following statements about Exercise 'Sea Vigil-22': 1. Its aim is to assess India's preparedness in the domain of Maritime security and coastal defence. 2. Naval forces of USA and Japan also took part in the exercise. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Exercise Sea Vigil is a national-level coastal defence exercise by the Indian Navy, so USA and Japan did not participate. Thus, only statement 1 is correct.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Consider the following statements: 1. England is the only country that won the ICC T20 World Cup twice. 2. Virat Kohli is the only player to be adjudged as the Player of the Series in the ICC T20 World Cup twice. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Both statements are correct based on records up to early 2023. West Indies also won it twice later, but in the context of the question's timeline, England had won it in 2010 and 2022.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Who among the following is not a recipient of Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2022?
explanation: Benjamin List won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2021, not 2022.

subject: Biology
text: Consider the following statements regarding cell wall composition: 1. Bacterial cell wall is made of peptidoglycan. 2. Fungal cell wall is made of cellulose. 3. Animals lack cell wall and have extracellular matrix made up of sugar and proteins. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Statement 2 is incorrect as fungal cell walls are made of chitin. Statements 1 and 3 are correct.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following structures is not present in a prokaryotic cell?
explanation: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

subject: Biology
text: In a plant cell, which one of the following contains their own DNA?
explanation: Mitochondria and Chloroplasts are semi-autonomous organelles that contain their own circular DNA.

subject: Biology
text: One advantage of sexual reproduction over asexual reproduction is that it helps species to survive over long evolutionary time. This is because sexual reproduction produces:
options: more offspring in each reproductive cycle.
explanation: Sexual reproduction generates genetic variation through recombination, enhancing the population's adaptability.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following structures or components is not always present in living cells?
explanation: Animal cells lack a cell wall, so it is not always present in all living cells.

subject: Chemistry
text: Browning of the chopped apple can be minimized by:
explanation: Lemon juice contains ascorbic acid (Vitamin C), an antioxidant that prevents the oxidation (browning) of apples.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which of the hydrocarbons are arranged as per the increasing order of their boiling points?
explanation: Boiling points of alkanes increase with molecular weight: Propane (C3) < Butane (C4) < Pentane (C5) < Octane (C8).

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following apparatus is used for separating benzene and water mixture?
explanation: A separating funnel is used to separate immiscible liquids like benzene and water based on density differences.

subject: Chemistry
text: An iron nail dipped in copper sulphate solution turns brown. This is due to which one of the following types of reactions?
explanation: Iron displaces copper from the copper sulphate solution because it is more reactive, forming a brown deposit of copper on the nail.

subject: Chemistry
text: Among the following, which is not the correct method for keeping the curd?
explanation: Curd contains lactic acid which reacts with copper, potentially forming toxic compounds.

subject: Chemistry
text: Toothpaste prevents tooth decay by:
options: making a coat of calcium over the teeth surface.
explanation: Toothpaste is generally basic in nature and neutralizes the acid produced by bacteria in the mouth.

subject: History
text: Freedom fighter Kanaklata Barua was martyred in:
explanation: Kanaklata Barua was shot dead by police during the Quit India Movement in 1942 while leading a procession bearing the National Flag.

subject: History
text: Which one among the following statements about the Mansabdari system is correct?
options: All army troopers were allotted mansabs.
Mansabs were usually assigned on the basis of ancestry.
Position and salary of mansabdars were indicated by a numerical designation called zat.
Mansabdars were never paid in cash.
explanation: In the Mansabdari system, 'zat' fixed the personal rank and salary of a Mansabdar.

subject: History
text: Which Governor General of Bengal underwent impeachment proceedings in the British Parliament?
explanation: Warren Hastings was impeached in the British Parliament for his administrative actions in India, though he was ultimately acquitted.

subject: History
text: Who among the following composed the 'Prayag Prashasti' of Samudragupta?
explanation: Harishena, the court poet of Samudragupta, composed the Prayag Prashasti (Allahabad Pillar Inscription).

subject: History
text: Which one of the following kingdoms was founded by the two brothers Harihar and Bukka?
explanation: The Vijayanagara Empire was established in 1336 by brothers Harihara I and Bukka Raya I.

subject: History
text: At which one of the following places did the Danes establish their settlement in India?
explanation: The Danes established their primary settlement at Tranquebar (Tharangambadi) in Tamil Nadu in 1620.

subject: Physics
text: A rectangle ABCD is kept in front of a concave mirror of focal length f with its corners A and B being, respectively, at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror with AB along the principal axis as shown in the figure. It forms an image A'B'C'D' in front of the mirror. What is the ratio of B'C' to A'D'?
explanation: Point A is at 2f, so its image A' is also at 2f with magnification -1 (A'D' = AD). Point B is at 3f, so its image B' is at 1.5f with magnification m = f/(f-3f) = -1/2. Thus B'C' = 0.5 BC. Since ABCD is a rectangle, AD = BC. The ratio of B'C' to A'D' is 1/2.

subject: Physics
text: Shown in the figure are two plane mirrors XY and YZ (XY ⊥ YZ) joined at their edge. Also shown is a light ray falling on one of the mirrors and reflected back parallel to its original path as a result of this arrangement. The two mirrors are now rotated by an angle θ to their new position X'YZ', as shown. As a result the new reflected ray is at an angle α from the original reflected ray. Then:
explanation: For two mutually perpendicular plane mirrors, the incident ray and the emergent reflected ray are always anti-parallel to each other regardless of the angle of incidence. Rotating the entire system by θ doesn't change this relative orientation, so α = 0.

subject: Physics
text: A railway wagon (open at the top) of mass M1 is moving with speed v1 along a straight track. As a result of rain, after some time it gets partially filled with water so that the mass of the wagon becomes M2 and speed becomes v2. Taking the rain to be falling vertically and water stationary inside the wagon, the relation between the two speeds v1 and v2 is:
explanation: There are no external horizontal forces, so the horizontal momentum is conserved. M1*v1 = M2*v2.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following statements is not true for a flute, a musical instrument?
options: Momentum of waves on the blowing jet determines the loudness of the produced note.
Arrival time of the waves on the blowing jet determines the pitch of the produced note.
Sound comes from a vibrating column of air inside the flute.
Sound comes from a vibrating column of air inside as well as outside the flute.
explanation: Sound in a flute originates specifically from the resonating, vibrating column of air inside the instrument tube.

subject: Physics
text: A positive charge is moving towards south in a space where magnetic field is pointing in the north direction. The moving charge will experience:
options: a deflecting force towards north direction.
a deflecting force towards east direction.
a deflecting force towards west direction.
explanation: The charge is moving South and the magnetic field is North. The angle is 180°. Force F = qvB sin(180°) = 0. Therefore, no force.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is not a main greenhouse gas?
explanation: Oxygen (O2) does not absorb infrared radiation and is not a greenhouse gas.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following statements about X-rays is not true?
options: They have wavelengths of about 1 Å.
These can be generated by bombarding a metal target by high energy electrons.
Due to their wavelengths being shorter, these can be used for radar systems.
These are also used for the treatment of certain forms of cancer.
explanation: Radar systems use radio waves and microwaves, not X-rays, because radar relies on the reflection of waves off macroscopic objects over long distances.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following is the northernmost geographical location?
explanation: New Delhi is at ~28.6°N, Kathmandu is ~27.7°N, Thimphu is ~27.4°N, and Dhaka is ~23.8°N.

subject: Geography
text: Consider the following statements: 1. Distance between the longitudes becomes zero on North Pole and South Pole. 2. Distance between the longitudes is maximum on the Equator. 3. Number of longitudes is more than number of latitudes. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Longitudes converge at poles (distance = 0) and are widest at the equator. There are 360 longitudes and 181 latitudes. All statements are correct.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following states is the leading producer of Manganese in India?
explanation: Madhya Pradesh is the leading manganese-producing state in India.

subject: Geography
text: Mica is mainly used in:
explanation: Mica is an excellent insulator and can withstand high voltage, making it indispensable in electrical and electronic industries.

subject: Geography
text: According to Koppen's climatic classifications, the Great Northern Plains of India have which one of the following climates?
explanation: Cwg (Monsoon type with dry winter) corresponds to the Great Northern Plains of India.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Which of the following statements about Lachit Borphukan is/are correct? 1. He was a General of the Ahom Force. 2. He is known for his leadership in the Battle of Saraighat. 3. Lachit Borphukan Gold Medal is given to the best cadet at the National Defence Academy. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: All three statements about the famous Ahom general Lachit Borphukan are true.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The 4th edition of joint military exercise 'DUSTLIK' between the Indian Army and the Uzbekistan Army was held in:
explanation: The 4th edition of the exercise DUSTLIK was held in Pithoragarh, Uttarakhand.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Which of the following is India's first privately developed rocket launched by ISRO recently?
explanation: Vikram-S, developed by Skyroot Aerospace, was India's first privately developed rocket to be launched.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Which one among the following villages was recently declared as India's first 24x7 solar-powered village?
explanation: Modhera in Gujarat was declared as India's first 24x7 solar-powered village.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Tableaux of which one of the following States was adjudged the best in the Republic Day Parade, 2023?
explanation: Uttarakhand's tableau, showcasing the state's wildlife and religious sites, won the first prize in 2023.

subject: Biology
text: Organisms capable of using CO2 as principal carbon source are called:
explanation: Autotrophs (like plants and some bacteria) can fix atmospheric carbon dioxide into organic compounds.

subject: Biology
text: When yeast cells are O2 starved, fermentation serves as the source of energy. This results in the production of:
explanation: In anaerobic conditions (O2 starved), yeast undergoes alcoholic fermentation, producing Ethanol, CO2, and ATP.

subject: Biology
text: During a laboratory experiment, a student immerses epidermal leaf peel in a hypertonic solution. After some time, the student examined the cells under a microscope and observed that:
options: the cells built up turgor pressure.
explanation: A hypertonic solution draws water out of the plant cells, causing the cell membrane to pull away from the cell wall, a process called plasmolysis.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of fungi?
options: Cell wall is made of chitin
explanation: Fungi lack chlorophyll and are heterotrophic, meaning they cannot carry out photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following statements about bryophytes is not correct?
options: The plant body is a gametophyte.
They are also called the amphibians of plant kingdom.
The plant body is attached to the substratum by rhizoids.
explanation: Bryophytes are non-vascular plants, meaning they lack specialized water-conducting tissues (xylem and phloem).

subject: Chemistry
text: What is the specific purpose of using potassium hydroxide during the saponification process?
options: To obtain soaps which are hard on the skin
To obtain soaps which are soft on the skin
To make the saponification very economical
explanation: Potassium hydroxide (KOH) is used instead of sodium hydroxide (NaOH) to produce liquid or softer soaps that are gentler on the skin.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is the correct arrangement of metals in the decreasing order of their reactivity?
explanation: Sodium is highly reactive, followed by Iron. Copper is less reactive, and Silver is least reactive among them.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which among the following statements is not correct with respect to allotropes of Carbon?
options: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity.
Diamond is the hardest substance known.
Fullerene is one of the allotropes of carbon.
Next to diamond, graphite is the second hardest known substance.
explanation: Graphite is soft and slippery because its layers can easily slide over one another. It is not the second hardest known substance.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which among the following is the popular method for manufacture of ammonia?
explanation: The Haber-Bosch process is the main industrial procedure for the production of ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen gases.

subject: Chemistry
text: For manufacturing of glass, which among the following is used as a source of silica?
explanation: Silica sand (silicon dioxide) is the primary component and source of silica in glass manufacturing.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which among the following is the correct arrangement of halogens in the increasing order of their oxidizing nature?
explanation: Oxidizing power increases up the group in halogens. Iodine is the weakest, and Fluorine is the strongest. Thus, I < Br < Cl < F.

subject: Chemistry
text: Copper sulphate crystals available in the market are blue coloured crystals. By careful heating, they turn to white colour. Which one of the following is responsible for the blue colour?
explanation: The blue colour of copper sulphate crystals is due to the presence of water of crystallization (CuSO4·5H2O). When heated, it loses water and turns white.

subject: Chemistry
text: Equal volume of all gases, when measured at the same temperature and pressure, contain an equal number of particles. Who proposed the above law?
explanation: Avogadro's law states that equal volumes of all gases, at the same temperature and pressure, have the same number of molecules.

subject: Chemistry
text: Airbags work on the principle of a chemical reaction triggered by the impact producing a gaseous product that causes a sudden volume change. Which one among the following chemical conversions is responsible for this?
options: Solid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide
Sudden conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide
explanation: Upon impact, sodium azide (NaN3) decomposes rapidly to produce sodium metal and nitrogen gas, which inflates the airbag.

subject: History
text: Which one among the following districts of Eastern India held a referendum in 1947 to decide whether or not to join Pakistan?
explanation: The Sylhet referendum was held in July 1947 to decide whether the district of Sylhet in Assam would remain in India or join East Bengal (Pakistan).

subject: History
text: Which one of the following statements about Rabatak inscription is not correct?
options: It throws important light on Kushana genealogy.
It refers to Kanishka as 'a king of kings and a son of God'.
The 23-line inscription is written in Gandhari language.
It mentions names of States which were part of Kanishka's empire.
explanation: The Rabatak inscription is written in the Bactrian language and Greek script, not Gandhari.

subject: History
text: Who among the following was the author of the famous Sanskrit work 'Mrichchhakatika'?
explanation: The Sanskrit play 'Mrichchhakatika' (The Little Clay Cart) was written by King Shudraka.

subject: History
text: Yashovarman was ruler of which one of the following kingdoms?
explanation: Yashovarman was a powerful king of Kannauj in the early 8th century.

subject: History
text: Which one among the following rulers established Pataliputra as the capital of the Magadhan Empire?
explanation: Ajatashatru (and his successor Udayin) built the fort at Pataligrama, which eventually became the capital Pataliputra.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following astronomers proved that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun?
explanation: Nicolaus Copernicus was the first to formulate a comprehensive heliocentric cosmology which displaced the Earth from the center of the universe.

subject: Physics
text: Sand falls vertically on a conveyor belt at a rate of 0.1 kg/s. In order to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s, the force required to be applied on the belt is:
explanation: Force F = v * (dm/dt) = 2 m/s * 0.1 kg/s = 0.2 N.

subject: Physics
text: The power required to lift a mass of 8.0 kg up a vertical distance of 4 m in 2 s is (taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s^2):
explanation: Work done W = mgh = 8 * 10 * 4 = 320 J. Power = W/t = 320 / 2 = 160 W.

subject: Physics
text: One block of 2.0 kg mass is placed on top of another block of 3.0 kg mass. The coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is 0.2. The bottom block is pulled with a horizontal force F such that both the blocks move together without slipping. Taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s^2, the maximum value of the frictional force is:
explanation: The maximum static frictional force between the blocks is f_max = mu * N = 0.2 * (2.0 kg * 10 m/s^2) = 0.2 * 20 = 4 N.

subject: Physics
text: A mass is attached to a spring that hangs vertically. The extension produced in the spring is 6 cm on Earth. The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Moon is one-sixth of its value on the surface of the Earth. The extension of the spring on the Moon would be:
explanation: Extension x = mg/k. Since g_moon = g_earth / 6, the extension on the moon will be 6 cm / 6 = 1 cm.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following is an example of Second Class Lever?
explanation: In a second-class lever, the load is located between the fulcrum and the effort (e.g., bottle opener, wheelbarrow).

subject: Physics
text: In an electric circuit, a wire of resistance 10 Ω is used. If this wire is stretched to a length double of its original value, the current in the circuit would become:
options: four times of its original value.
explanation: When a wire is stretched to double its length, its area halves. New resistance R' = rho(2L)/(A/2) = 4 * R. Since resistance becomes 4 times, current becomes one-fourth.

subject: Physics
text: What is the total resistance in the following circuit element? [Three resistors each of R arranged in a specific way, usually parallel or series combinations. Based on common questions with 'R', 'R', 'R' in a triangle/bridge]:
explanation: For three equal resistors R in parallel, it's R/3. If two are in parallel and one in series, it's 1.5 R. Without the image, a common structure resulting in '2R/3' is two in series in parallel with one (2R || R = 2R/3).

subject: General Knowledge
text: Which of the following scheme(s) is/are included under Bharatmala Pariyojana? 1. Develop the road connectivity to border areas 2. Development of coastal roads 3. Improvement in the efficiency of National Corridors Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: Bharatmala Pariyojana is an umbrella program for the highways sector that focuses on optimizing efficiency, coastal road development, and border connectivity.

subject: Geography
text: Deendayal Port was earlier known as:
explanation: Kandla Port in Gujarat was renamed as Deendayal Port in 2017.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The Counter Insurgency and Jungle Warfare School of Indian Army is situated at:
explanation: The Counter Insurgency and Jungle Warfare School (CIJWS) is located in Vairengte, Mizoram.

subject: Geography
text: In the soil-forming regime, which one of the following occurs in a region where evapotranspiration exceeds precipitation significantly?
explanation: Calcification occurs in arid and semi-arid regions where evapotranspiration exceeds precipitation, bringing calcium carbonate to the upper soil layers.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is found in the innermost part of the Earth?
explanation: Lehmann discontinuity separates the liquid outer core from the solid inner core, making it the innermost.

subject: Geography
text: Which of the following tree species is/are found on Himalayas? 1. Oak 2. Rhododendron 3. Rosewood Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: Oak and Rhododendron are typical of the Himalayan temperate forests. Rosewood is a tropical hardwood.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is the lowermost/innermost intrusive igneous rock?
explanation: Batholiths are massive intrusive igneous rock bodies that form deep within the Earth's crust.

subject: Geography
text: If it is 12 noon in New Delhi, what will be the time in London, UK?
explanation: IST is GMT+5:30. Therefore, London (GMT) is 5 hours and 30 minutes behind New Delhi. 12:00 Noon - 5:30 = 6:30 A.M.

subject: Geography
text: Which of the following positions of Sun, Earth and Moon is/are suitable for Spring Tide? 1. SYZYGY Conjunction 2. SYZYGY Opposition 3. Quadrature Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: Spring tides occur when the Sun, Earth, and Moon are aligned (Syzygy), which includes both conjunction (new moon) and opposition (full moon).

subject: History
text: Which Renaissance artist painted 'The Last Supper'?
explanation: 'The Last Supper' is a famous late 15th-century mural painting by Leonardo da Vinci.

subject: History
text: Which one of the following European explorer conquered Mexico?
explanation: Hernán Cortés was a Spanish conquistador who led an expedition that caused the fall of the Aztec Empire in Mexico.

subject: History
text: Near which one of the following cities in India have large statues of Kushana rulers been discovered?
explanation: Mathura was a major center for Kushana art, and several large, headless statues of Kushana kings (like Kanishka) have been found near Mathura (at Mat).

subject: History
text: The Treaty of Yandabo was concluded as part of which one of the following wars?
explanation: The Treaty of Yandabo (1826) ended the First Anglo-Burmese War.

subject: Economy
text: Plan allocation in agriculture and irrigation as percentage of total plan outlay was highest in:
explanation: The First Five-Year Plan (1951-56) focused predominantly on agriculture and irrigation, having the highest percentage allocation to these sectors.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The UN COP-27 Summit relates to:
explanation: COP-27 is the 27th United Nations Climate Change conference.

subject: History
text: Which one of the following tribes from India's North-East had the earliest known association with cultivation and production of tea?
explanation: The Singpho tribe in Assam were the earliest known cultivators of tea in India.

subject: Polity
text: By which one of the following amendments, was Article 51A, relating to the Fundamental Duties, inserted into the Constitution of India?
explanation: Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following statements is true?
options: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon is greater than the force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth.
The force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth is greater than the force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude and are in the same direction.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.
explanation: By Newton's third law of motion, the gravitational force exerted by the Earth on the Moon is equal in magnitude and opposite in direction to the force exerted by the Moon on the Earth.

subject: Physics
text: An electric bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. When it is operated on 110 V, the power rating would be:
explanation: Power P = V^2 / R. When voltage is halved (110 V instead of 220 V), the power consumed becomes (1/2)^2 = 1/4 of the original power. 80 W / 4 = 20 W.

subject: Physics
text: In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, which one among the following is correct?
options: Red light deviates the most because red light has highest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has highest speed in prism
Red light deviates the most because red light has lowest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has lowest speed in prism
explanation: Blue light bends (deviates) the most because its speed is the lowest in glass compared to red light (having a shorter wavelength and higher refractive index).

subject: Physics
text: Which one among the following is true for the speed of sound in a given medium?
options: Speed of sound remains same at all frequencies
Speed of sound is faster at higher frequencies
Speed of sound is slower at higher frequencies
Speed of sound is slower at higher wavelengths
explanation: The speed of sound in a given medium is a property of the medium and is largely independent of frequency.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following telescopes contains only mirrors?
explanation: The Newtonian telescope is a type of reflecting telescope that uses only mirrors (a concave primary mirror and a flat diagonal secondary mirror).
